_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL Hillsborough/Pinellas House batch HD62-66 (Rayner, Hart, Valdes, Gonzalez Pittman, Koster). Evidence-first; party-inference cells nulled; cross-party deviations flagged.

set.office: State Representative (HD-62 sitting; 2026 candidate for FL Senate District 16)

profile.background: Attorney (JD, Florida Coastal); former local counsel for NAACP Legal Defense Fund. First openly queer Black lesbian member of the Florida Legislature (elected 2020). Not seeking FL House reelection in 2026; running for FL Senate District 16 to succeed term-limited Sen. Darryl Rouson.

michele-k-rayner@FL.notes_set: Consistent progressive record. HARD contrary evidence: Planned Parenthood PAC endorsement (life), Equality Florida endorsement and self-identified LGBTQ-rights advocacy (marriage), co-sponsored HB73 to REPEAL Stand Your Ground and publicly opposed FL permitless-carry HB543 (2A). No conservative cross-party deviation found. Religion not publicly documented. CURRENCY: vacating HD-62 to run for FL Senate SD-16 in 2026.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by Florida Planned Parenthood PAC.

0.note: Openly queer legislator and Equality Florida-endorsed LGBTQ-rights advocate; opposes the conservative marriage position.

2.note: Floor opposition to GOP anti-trans/LGBTQ bills ('I'm literally trying to exist').

4.note: Opposed 'Don't Say Gay'/anti-LGBTQ school measures; Equality Florida endorsee.

2.note: Opposed restrictions on LGBTQ instruction; FEA + Equality Florida endorsed.

3.note: Co-sponsored HB73 to REPEAL Stand Your Ground.

profile.background: Dianne 'Ms Dee' Hart-Lowman (b. 1955, Tampa). FL House since 2018 (HD-61, redistricted to HD-63 in 2022); won reelection Nov 2024. Signature issue: prison reform and reentry (parole/gain-time expansion, inmates' bill of rights).

dianne-hart-lowman@FL.notes_set: Consistent progressive record representing a majority-minority Tampa district. HARD contrary evidence: Planned Parenthood-affiliated endorsement (life), Equality Florida PAC endorsement (marriage), and sponsorship of parole/gain-time/sentence-reduction bills (HB189/HB115/HB185) which run opposite the rubric's proportional-punishment/oppose-soft-on-crime asks. No conservative cross-party deviation found. Party-line inferences left null per evidence rule.

4.note: Equality Florida PAC endorsee; supports LGBTQ policy.

1.note: Sponsors parole/gain-time expansion to release inmates earlier — leniency posture.

2.note: Sentence-reduction/reform posture rather than proportional punishment.

profile.background: First-gen Cuban-American; former medical-practice administrator and Hillsborough school board member. Elected to FL House 2018 as a Democrat; switched to the Republican Party Dec 2024 (giving the House GOP an 86-34 supermajority). Term-limited; HD-64 is an open seat in 2026.

susan-l-valdes@FL.notes_set: Party switch Democrat->Republican confirmed Dec 2024. CURRENCY: term-limited, NOT on the 2026 ballot (open seat). Documented conservative votes: HB1 universal school-choice vouchers (YES, 2023 — one of ~3 Democrats to do so) and SB2C immigration enforcement (YES, 2025, her first major vote as a Republican). Documented contrary (Democratic-era): Planned Parenthood and Equality Florida PAC endorsements. The widely-circulated claim that she voted YES on the 6-week ban (SB300) could not be confirmed in any roll call and contradicts her endorsements — left null per evidence rule.

0.note: Equality Florida PAC + LGBTQ+ Democratic Caucus endorsee (Democratic era).
Voted YES on HB1 universal school-choice voucher expansion (2023) — one of only ~3 Democrats to do so.

2.note: Voted YES on SB2C 2025 immigration-enforcement package (local ICE cooperation, State Immigration Enforcement Council).

profile.background: Former Hillsborough/Pinellas schoolteacher and program evaluator; M.Ed., Univ. of South Florida. FL House since 2022. Running for reelection 2026. Florida Freedom Index cumulative ~61% (low for a Republican).

karen-gonzalez-pittman@FL.notes_set: DEVIATION ALERT: despite an R label, documented votes break left on top RESOLUTE issues. Voted NO on the 6-week abortion ban SB300 (2023, one of only 7 House Republicans against) and NO on a fetal-personhood bill H289 (2026); reportedly NO on HB1 universal school choice (Freedom Index). Reliably conservative on anti-ESG (HB3), constitutional-money/financial-privacy (HB999/SB214), backing police (HB601 limiting civilian review boards), and immigration enforcement (HB1451 denying ID cards to illegal aliens). Marriage, Christian liberty, election integrity, and most 2A specifics: no individual-vote evidence found -> null.

1.note: Voted NO on the 6-week abortion ban SB300 (2023) — one of only 7 House Republicans against.

0.note: Voted NO on HB1 universal school-choice/voucher expansion (2023) per Florida Freedom Index.
Voted YES on constitutional-money HB999 and 2A financial-privacy SB214 (anti-financial-surveillance).
Voted YES on HB601 restricting civilian police review boards (backed law enforcement).

2.note: Voted YES on HB1451 denying ID cards to illegal aliens (immigration enforcement).

4.note: Voted YES on SB214 2A financial privacy (opposes financial-surveillance/ATF-style overreach).

profile.background: Family-law attorney (JD, Stetson). FL House since 2020 (HD-64, redistricted to HD-66 in 2022). Running for reelection 2026. NRA-endorsed (2022). John Birch Society Freedom Index lifetime ~63%.

traci-koster@FL.notes_set: Conservative on the votes that exist: YES on both abortion bans (HB5 15-week 2022, SB300 6-week 2023), YES on HB1557 Parental Rights in Education, YES on HB3 anti-ESG, YES on SB524 Office of Election Crimes & Security, pro-gun (HB1543 lowering purchase age, SB214 financial privacy; NRA-endorsed). DEVIATION: voted NO on HB1 universal school choice (2023) per Freedom Index. Public Justice, Refuse Federal Overreach, most Christian Liberty/Marriage/Life-personhood: no individual-vote evidence found -> null (prior scorecard's blanket TRUEs were party-inference).

1.note: Voted YES on HB5 (15-week ban, 2022) and SB300 (6-week ban, 2023) — abortion restrictions.

4.note: Voted YES on HB1557 Parental Rights in Education (limits classroom SOGI instruction).

0.note: Voted NO on HB1 universal school choice (2023) per Freedom Index.

1.note: Voted YES on HB1557 Parental Rights in Education (parental notification on instruction).

2.note: Voted YES on HB1557 limiting SOGI/sex-ed instruction in early grades.

4.note: Voted YES on SB524 Office of Election Crimes & Security (2022).
NRA-endorsed; YES on SB214 2A financial privacy (opposes financial-surveillance/ATF-style overreach).
